Sarnak Summoners Staff  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Charges: Unlimited
Skill: 2H Blunt Atk Delay: 48
DMG: 20 Dmg Bonus: 162
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
DEX: +10 INT: +15 AGI: +10 MANA: +100
Effect: Summon Lava Diamond (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 15.0) at Level 50
WT: 6.5 Size: LARGE
Class: MAG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55

▲ Page top
Bards...
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2001 at 10:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591768097152
7 posts
Actually mages summon these and give them to bards... who in turn cast them on other people. If a bard casts it, the effect will stack with every other damage shield out there, including the mage DS.

So yes this is an extreamly useful item.
▲ Page top
RE: Bards...
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2001 at 11:13 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361715796
51 posts
This changed 2 patches ago. Bards now use items just like everyone else, and their use no longer has any innate bard stacking privaleges.
▲ Page top
hello?
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2001 at 9:57 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592790011884
33 posts
Its for major melee targets. People who constantly question stuff are the many who never do crap in this game. They join some lame guild who cant even kill a kunark dragon.

Its very simple, you fight a straight melee target like statue of rallos zek and you have a 150+ point dmg sheild on hes gonna beat the heck out of himself on the dmg shield. THATs why this staff is so nice, its a major raid component that obviously some will never need.
Gets old listening to people argue and flame an item simply because "they" dont need it. They simply lack the experience in the high end game to figure out an items true purpose
▲ Page top
RE: hello?
QuoteReply
# Oct 09 2001 at 7:03 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Just to play Devils Advocate, ;)

If you need to actually label something negatively (lame) you've got issues. Labels are the weakest attempt to convince someone of your "opinion" instead of use hard facts and data to convince your target audience.

You actually care whether someone should loot because YOU believe they need it? Well as soon Verant establishes a loot committe based on NEED I'll be sure to submit your name as a candidate (heheh yeah right)

What actually gets old is people who's self importance is measured by their gratifying belief in Uberness. They sit here filled to the brim in self importance because they in their eyes they can play "better" then others, they belong to guilds who kill gods, dragons and other Uber Nasties.

Here's a little insight, everyone is entitled to play for their own reasons. Everyone is entitled to have anything this game has to offer. Everyone is entitled to decide what in this "game" gives them pleasure and what displeases them. The simple proof of this is as soon as Verant STOPS charging a fee to play and they (the creators of the game) decide how your suppose to play it, (last time I checked their isn't a printed GOAL for this game) then and ONLY then will there be a guide as to Who can have what item (regardless if you can equip or not). The purpose for playing this game is (to reach Uberness, make friends, socialize, escape reality, be an *** with little chance of harm, show the world how immature you can be, show the world how ignorant you can be, etc, etc, etc) decide by each individual. Noone has the right to tell anyone else what their purpose is.

In the end (this game will end one day) you will leave with nothing, you will have accomplished nothing, you will have nothing to show for all the time you invested in this game IF you haven't utilized it's social enviroment to make long lasting friendships.

This is of course moot if you don't care about making lasting friendships and just play this game for the sheer fun of it and to pass time in mindless enjoyment.

To each his own.
▲ Page top
RE: hello?
QuoteReply
# Apr 29 2002 at 4:09 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965792734
105 posts
Dear mister anonymous Devil's Advocate.

You missed the point totally. You can do whatever you want in EQ, just don't post pointless crap about stuff you'll never see anyway. Get it?

The poor shammy you bashed in an oh so intelligent way was even nice enough to explain the use of the item in question.

Analyze the social aspects of EQ elsewhere please.

Well Cantiz. Not everyone in this game wants to be Uber. Some of us join guilds because we like to hang out with friends. And just because these guilds may lack the member numbers to kill a kunark dragon doesnt mean they are lame.

From what I have seen the people with the NEED to be uber and calling players who dont have this need lame, 99% are below the age of 20, most likelly with some serious problems getting respect in RL...

▲ Page top
RE: hello?
QuoteReply
# Oct 09 2001 at 5:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592790011884
33 posts
And your point is taken, but since this drops in VP how do you expect them to get it? Enter the orginized "uber" guild. Sorry but in the end this game comes down to lrg guilds to play the high end.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 10 2001 at 11:24 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) geezes Cantiz you are a dumb **** man, its not about larg guilds well partly but not all i am in a guild that is not even ready for this yet but yet i get picked to go on VP raids alot accually its about your reputation if you have a good one and people hear good things about you chances are you will get picked for these raids and may get one of these items like up here, but for ignorance on my server doesn't go far and people in EQ hate ingnorance, if your in a guild then you are putting a bad name for them as you were writing before. i have seen more lots of people not even in a guild get uber items. this is just a game no reasons for the ignorance
RE: hello?
QuoteReply
# Oct 09 2001 at 6:34 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592249498837
15 posts
well said..Uber is usually more like Goober, my guild doesn't regularly raid VP. What we do is hang out and look after one another, all part of "enjoying the game" and not setting out to "ownz joo all". There are many guilds out there like ours and we tend to find each other when the task at hand calls for a large party. In that respect i would like to thank appropriate people ... DFL =)

i am also on bertoxx, i am also a monk, and i also in a guild thats looks out for 1 another. (sentinels of grimspire)i think it is time for Cantiz to shut up plp r in guilds for different reasons, the plp that need to be the best r in "uber" guilds, while the social ones are in guilds that do more comunity service and social kinda things, like scavenger hunts. i think that every1 has a different PoV and this is not worth argueing over

# Oct 08 2001 at 5:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591769487623
Scholar
30 posts
This would explain the high level mage i saw the other day in the Freeport newb yard.

He was walking around and kept stopping by the newbies. A few seconds later the newbs would glow. Saw it like 3 times. I wasn't close enough to read the actual wording on the spell they were using, but given this i'd wager the guy summoned a few of these stones to help the newbies clean house.

It worked by the way. On the way out i don't think i saw 1 frikkin MoB till the edge of FP/EC.

# Oct 09 2001 at 3:22 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592341837046
View User Forum
Scholar
582 posts
Not necessarily. Mages have good Damage Shield spells available anyway, so tossing on a DS on a newbie can help -- but only if the newbie can hit the creature before it kills itself on the damage shield. Otherwise, it poofs, no experience. Quite frustrating for the newbie....
▲ Page top
Usefulness...
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2001 at 5:34 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593966037382
69 posts
This item appears to be quite useful... Even though it interferes with stein, it has 15 int and 100 mana of its own. Also, just because you are using an items effect (summon lava diamond) doesnt mean you would have to keep it equiped. Example: Mage with this staff summons 100 lava diamonds for different people, starting 1 hour before a raid. Everyone has the lava diamond who needs one. Mage removes staff and equips the alternative. When a mob that casts an AE fire spell is going to be engaged, everyone uses their lava diamond, and the fight begins.

This would have limited use though. The lava diamond would only be used once per raid if you wanted to give one to every person participating. Although, 20 in a resistance can be the differance in 500 damage that kills you and 400 that doesnt.
▲ Page top
RE: Usefulness...
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2001 at 7:19 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591768204909
372 posts
This is not a resistance item, the summoned item that is. It is a dmg shield item.

# Oct 08 2001 at 8:10 PM Rating: Decent

9 posts
The Dmg Shield also gives fire resistance
▲ Page top
Lava Diamonds are...
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2001 at 4:55 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591762879563
5 posts
This stave summons a LORE + NO-RENT item with one charge of the lvl 49 Magician spell "Shield of Lava". The Lava Diamond can then be handed to anyone, and is useable by anyone.

"Shield of Lava" is a 25-pt damage shield that lasts for about 5 minutes.

The stave's usefulness has been debated on the Mage Compendium for some time... its nice at first glance (you save mana), but:
1. The delay to make one diamond is longer than the time it takes to cast the spell that is on the diamond
2. Its a 2H item which interferes with more useful INT items such as the Stein, etc.
▲ Page top
Lava Diamonds
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2001 at 4:53 PM Rating: Excellent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Lava Diamonds are unstackable, lore, norent items that give a ~5-minute damage shield and 25 resist fire bonus similar to some mage damage shields. You have to target yourself when using the diamond. They're fairly handy when fighting a fire-based ae. Prior to the fight the mage can drop a couple dozen for people to pick up. Mage DS's the big tanks and everyone else munches one of these when the mob is incoming.

The staff looks like the cow skull ontop of a semi-translucent staff.
▲ Page top
